prompts.proposer:
Call proposer_brief() FIRST. The tool output includes pattern_analyst_insights — use trend_champion, seasonality_champion, and insights.recommended_method_hint to bias your candidate selection toward non-mean methods. MUST propose at least 3 candidates including at least 1 non-baseline type. Return ONLY JSON per instructions. IMPORTANT: you MUST ONLY reference candidate names that appear in the tool output candidate_library; unknown names will hard-stop.
PATTERN ANALYST INSIGHTS: trend_champion='DWT_catboost', seasonality_champion='THETA', recommended_method='dba_combination'. Narrative: Analysis reveals high model disagreement (rmse_spread_ratio=0.48) and very high seasonality variance (0.76), indicating models capture patterns very differently. DWT_catboost leads trend tracking while THETA dominates seasonality capture. FT_rf is the overall best performer but early/late horizon specialists differ, suggesting DBA combination with mixed weighting is optimal to leverage complementary strengths across model types.
prompts.skeptic:

IMPORTANT: you MUST ONLY use candidate names from valid_candidate_names; unknown names hard-stop.
You may only remove/override candidates that are in current_candidate_names (or candidates you are adding).

raw.pattern_analyst:
{
  "trend_champion": "DWT_catboost",
  "seasonality_champion": "THETA",
  "overall_champion": "FT_rf",
  "horizon_specialists": {"early": "THETA", "late": "FT_rf"},
  "tier1_models": ["FT_rf", "THETA", "ETS", "ONLY_DWT_catboost", "CWT_catboost", "DWT_rf", "FT_catboost", "ONLY_CWT_rf"],
  "tier2_models": ["ONLY_CWT_svr", "DWT_catboost", "DWT_svr", "ONLY_DWT_rf", "CWT_rf", "ONLY_CWT_catboost", "FT_svr", "ARIMA"],
  "recommended_method_hint": "dba_combination",
  "recommended_weighting_basis": "mixed",
  "key_insights": {
    "rmse_spread_ratio": 0.4823,
    "high_disagreement": true,
    "high_seasonality_variance": true,
    "ytrue_trend_directions": ["up", "up", "up"]
  },
  "cot_narrative": "Analysis reveals high model disagreement (rmse_spread_ratio=0.48) and very high seasonality variance (0.76), indicating models capture patterns very differently. DWT_catboost leads trend tracking while THETA dominates seasonality capture. FT_rf is the overall best performer but early/late horizon specialists differ, suggesting DBA combination with mixed weighting is optimal to leverage complementary strengths across model types."
}
raw.proposer:
{
  "selected_names": ["dba_combination", "best_per_horizon_by_validation", "topk_mean_per_horizon_k5", "inverse_rmse_weights_k5_sh0.35", "exp_weighted_average_eta1.0_trim1.0"],
  "params_overrides": {"topk_mean_per_horizon_k5": {"top_k": 4}, "inverse_rmse_weights_k5_sh0.35": {"top_k": 4, "shrinkage": 0.3}},
  "score_preset": "balanced",
  "force_debate": false,
  "debate_margin": 0.02,
  "rationale": "n_unique_winners=9 indicates high horizon heterogeneity favoring per-horizon selection methods. RMSE spread ratio=0.48 suggests significant model disagreement, making dba_combination (recommended_method_hint) ideal for handling phase-shifted predictions. FT_rf (RMSE=2696.19) vs ONLY_FT_catboost (RMSE=3804.75) shows ~41% gap between best/worst models, justifying top-k selection over full mean. ETS appears in both trend_champions and seasonality_champions lists, supporting its inclusion via best_per_horizon_by_validation. shrinkage=0.3 added to inverse_rmse_weights for stability with only n_windows=3."
}
raw.skeptic:
{
  "add_names": [],
  "remove_names": [],
  "params_overrides": {},
  "rationale": "Current set has diverse types: baseline (dba_combination), selection (best_per_horizon_by_validation, topk_mean_per_horizon_k5), weighted (exp_weighted_average_eta1.0_trim1.0, inverse_rmse_weights_k5_sh0.35). No leakage detected - all candidates use anti-leakage rolling/expanding selection with n_windows=3 and expanding train_window=5. Diversity is sufficient (2 types: baseline+selection+weighted). Top candidate dba_combination wins 6/12 horizons for RMSE, 5/12 for MAPE. No pruning needed - set size=5 is appropriate.",
  "changes": [],
  "when_good": ""
}

pattern_analyst.cot_narrative: Analysis reveals high model disagreement (rmse_spread_ratio=0.48) and very high seasonality variance (0.76), indicating models capture patterns very differently. DWT_catboost leads trend tracking while THETA dominates seasonality capture. FT_rf is the overall best performer but early/late horizon specialists differ, suggesting DBA combination with mixed weighting is optimal to leverage complementary strengths across model types.

proposer.rationale: n_unique_winners=9 indicates high horizon heterogeneity favoring per-horizon selection methods. RMSE spread ratio=0.48 suggests significant model disagreement, making dba_combination (recommended_method_hint) ideal for handling phase-shifted predictions. FT_rf (RMSE=2696.19) vs ONLY_FT_catboost (RMSE=3804.75) shows ~41% gap between best/worst models, justifying top-k selection over full mean. ETS appears in both trend_champions and seasonality_champions lists, supporting its inclusion via best_per_horizon_by_validation. shrinkage=0.3 added to inverse_rmse_weights for stability with only n_windows=3.

skeptic.rationale: Current set has diverse types: baseline (dba_combination), selection (best_per_horizon_by_validation, topk_mean_per_horizon_k5), weighted (exp_weighted_average_eta1.0_trim1.0, inverse_rmse_weights_k5_sh0.35). No leakage detected - all candidates use anti-leakage rolling/expanding selection with n_windows=3 and expanding train_window=5. Diversity is sufficient (2 types: baseline+selection+weighted). Top candidate dba_combination wins 6/12 horizons for RMSE, 5/12 for MAPE. No pruning needed - set size=5 is appropriate.
